channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Federal employees may get more demands to justify their work at Elon Musk's direction
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The turmoil that enveloped the federal workforce over the last few days is unlikely to cease anytime soon as the U.S. government's human resources agency considers how to fulfill Elon Musk ’s demands.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Federal watchdog releases first DOGE-era report detailing areas of government prone to fraud, waste and abuse
item.description: The Government Accountability Office (GAO) comptroller general will testify before the House Oversight Committee on government areas vulnerable to fraud.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A Biden-era legal win paved the way for Trump's Kennedy Center board firings
item.description: Former Trump Press Secretary Sean Spicer detailed his lawsuit against the Biden administration with Fox News Digital over his own board removal that he says bolsters the Trump administration amid the Kennedy Center terminations debacle.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Feud between New York's governor and her No. 2 boils over
item.description: ALBANY, N.Y. (AP) — A simmering feud between New York Gov. Kathy Hochul and her handpicked lieutenant governor, Antonio Delgado, has boiled over after months of discord between the two Democrats culminated in what looked like a big breakup.
item.dc:creator: ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge declines to restore AP access to White House amid 'Gulf of America' dispute
item.description: A federal judge on Monday declined to restore The Associated Press’s access to certain spaces open to the media at the White House, including the Oval Office amid the Trump administration’s battle against the wire service over its refusal to use “Gulf of America” in its popular stylebook. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Judge blocks 2 federal agencies from disclosing personal records to Trump adviser Musk's DOGE
item.description: GREENBELT, Md. (AP) — A judge agreed Monday to temporarily bar two federal agencies from disclosing records containing sensitive personal information to representatives of billionaire Trump adviser Elon Musk's Department of Government Efficiency. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court turns down chance to claw back abortion clinic buffer zones
item.description: The Supreme Court turned down an opportunity to overturn its precedent permitting buffer zones around abortion clinics over the objections of two of the court’s leading conservatives. In two orders issued Monday, the court declined to take up challenges to ordinances in Carbondale, Ill.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court turns back challenges to laws keeping abortion opponents away from clinics, patients
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court refused Monday to hear a pair of cases from abortion opponents who say laws limiting anti-abortion demonstrations near clinics violate their First Amendment rights.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump posts SpongeBob meme mocking federal workers after email requesting accomplishments
item.description: President Donald Trump posted a SpongeBob meme mocking federal employees in the wake of an email sent to the workforce asking them to document their achievements in the past week.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, Fox 5

item.title: Hegseth defends Trump's firings of Pentagon leaders and says there may be more dismissals
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth insists President Donald Trump ’s abrupt firing of the nation’s senior military officer amid a wave of dismissals at the Pentagon wasn't unusual, brushing aside outcry that the new administration is openly seeking to inject politics into the military.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump spars with Maine's governor at the White House over transgender athletes
item.description: President Donald Trump sparred with Maine's Democratic governor during a meeting of governors at the White House on Friday, with Gov. Janet Mills telling the Republican president, “We'll see you in court,” over his push to deny federal funding to the state over transgender athletes.
item.dc:creator: Michelle L. Price, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Here's what to know about Germany's election this Sunday
item.description: An economic slump, an immigration crisis and the lifting of a security blanket provided for decades by the U.S. are issues on the minds of German voters. The far-right AfD is polling in second place.
item.dc:creator: Rob Schmitz, MPR News

item.title: Union workers written up, fired for using sick days; Nevada lawmaker moves to close loophole
item.description: Some union workers are afraid to use sick days because they might be written up or fired for being "undependable," and now a Nevada lawmaker is coming to their aid.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: US Marshals Service deputizes Musk's security team: Reports
item.description: The U.S. Marshals Service has deputized some of tech billionaire Elon Musk's private security detail, giving them specific rights and protections of federal law enforcement agents, multiple outlets reported.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, Fox 5

item.title: DeSantis reappoints Moms for Liberty cofounder to Florida ethics commission
item.description: Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis is attempting to put Moms for Liberty cofounder Tina Descovich on the Florida Commission on Ethics for the second time.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Gov. Hochul won't remove NYC Mayor Eric Adams, but pushes to limit powers
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – New York City Mayor Eric Adams will remain in office as Gov. Kathy Hochul pushes to implement "certain guardrails" to help protect New Yorkers. Hochul cited the “democratic process” as why she chose not to remove Adams from office.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Planned medical research grant cuts worry Sarasota scientists
item.description: For more than 20 years, Sarasota-based biomedical research facility The Roskamp Institute has been focused on understanding and finding treatment, prevention, and early detection for Alzheimer's disease and other dementia, along with illnesses facing veterans.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Florida sues Target for 'defrauding investors' by selling LGBTQ+ Pride merchandise
item.description: Florida Attorney General James Uthmeier on Thursday announced the state filed a class action lawsuit against Target claiming "the company knowingly misled and defrauded investors by concealing the financial risks of its radical LGBTQ activism.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, Fox 5

item.title: Cortez Masto, Rosen introduce proposal to block Trump plan to end birthright citizenship
item.description: Nevada Democratic Sens. Catherine Cortez Masto and Jacky Rosen introduced a proposal Thursday to block President Donald Trump's attempt to end the constitutional right to birthright citizenship.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Former NFL player Chris Kluwe arrested while protesting MAGA sign at city council meeting
item.description: Former NFL punter Chris Kluwe was detained at a city council meeting in California on Tuesday after delivering an impassioned speech criticizing the design of a plaque to be installed at the Huntington Beach Central Library.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, Fox 5

item.title: Trump may buy used planes to serve as Air Force One amid Boeing delays
item.description: It's not the planes, rather the heavy modification to make them suitable for the requirements of presidential travel and the top-level security clearances required for those involved, that has added to the cost and delays.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Florida Democrats file bill to repeal 6-week abortion ban
item.description: House Minority Leader Fentrice Driskell, who represents families in Hillsborough County, said the legislation comes because it’s a mandate from voters.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Nevada Democrats look to enshrine right to IVF in state law
item.description: Nevada Democrats introduced a proposal Wednesday to create a state law protecting the right to in vitro fertilization (IVF).
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: New York rep. says Trump has committed 'dozens of impeachable offenses' so far
item.description: “Donald Trump has, in his first month, probably committed dozens of impeachable offenses,” Rep. Dan Goldman (D-N.Y.) said in a recent interview.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Gov. Gavin Newsom proposes $125M mortgage relief program to help victims of recent natural disasters
item.description: Gov. Gavin Newsom on Wednesday proposed a new $125 million package to help residents whose homes were destroyed or damaged by recent natural disasters, including the recent wildfires.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Florida cabinet assumes new role, sparking criticism over privacy and power
item.description: After a month-long feud over who would be the state's chief immigration enforcement officer, state lawmakers passed a bill package that grants not one, but four elected officials with that task.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC
